title: Force-bond
content:
{{Top}}
{{Update|[[The High Republic: A Test of Courage]], [[The High Republic: Mission to Disaster]], [[Master & Apprentice]], [[Star Wars: Episode IX The Rise of Skywalker]], [[Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: Expanded Edition]]}}
{{ForcePower
|image=[[File:Force-bond.png]]
|name=Force-bond
|alternate=
|alignment=
|area=
|purpose=Allows [[Force-sensitive]]s to communicate with each other across great distances, sharing what they see and feel through [[the Force]]<ref name="Secrets of the Jedi" />
|inventor=[[Darth Sidious]] {{C|recreated the bond between [[Dooku]] and [[Yoda]]}}<ref name="Sacrifice">{{TCW|Sacrifice (episode)}}</ref>
|affiliation=*[[Jedi Order]]<ref name="Sacrifice" />
*[[Knights of Ren]]<ref name="Episode VIII">{{Film|VIII}}</ref>
*[[Sith]]<ref name="Sacrifice" />
}}
{{Quote|I feel the conflict in you. It's tearing you apart.|Rey, to Kylo Ren|<ref name="Episode VIII" />}}
A '''Force-bond''', also known as a '''Force connection''' or simply a '''bond''', was a powerful connection that existed between two [[Force-sensitive]] individuals. At times, [[the Force]] allowed a [[Species|living being]] to connect and communicate with another across great distances. On rare occasions, such a bond was even capable of being used to teleport matter. Despite its advantages, a bond could be manipulated by those using the [[dark side of the Force]]. A strong Force user had the power to initiate secret bonds with others in order to manipulate them. Even if the unwanted bond was detected, it could still be extremely difficult to break.

During the [[Clone Wars]], the [[Sith Lord]]s [[Darth Sidious]] and [[Dooku|Darth Tyranus]] performed a [[Dark illusion|dark ritual]] targeting [[Grand Master]] [[Yoda]] of the [[Jedi Order]]. As a result, the bond that once existed between Tyranus and his former [[Jedi Master|mentor]] was restored, but Yoda's will proved too strong for the [[Sith]] to break. After the [[Galactic Empire]] rose to [[Imperial Era|power]], [[Padawan]] [[Ezra Bridger]] became psychically linked to the former Sith [[Lord]] [[Maul]], a side effect of the [[Holocron fusion|fusion]] between a [[Kanan Jarrus' holocron|Jedi holocron]] and a [[Sith holocron (Malachor)|Sith holocron]]. Bridger consequently experienced a series of aural hallucinations and [[Force vision|visions]] that lasted until Maul's [[death]].

A unique bond existed between the [[Knights of Ren|dark warrior]] [[Ben Solo|Kylo Ren]] and the [[Jakku]] [[scavenger]] [[Rey Skywalker|Rey]] during the [[First Order-Resistance War]]. As the maternal [[Skywalker family|grandson]] of [[Anakin Skywalker|Darth Vader]] and the paternal [[Palpatine family|granddaughter]] of [[Darth]] Sidious respectively, both Ren and Rey formed a prophesied [[Force dyad]]. Due to their innate connection, they were able to communicate with each other across [[light-year]]s. After the [[Battle of Oetchi|death]] of [[Supreme Leader]] [[Snoke]], Ren and Rey fought as one against the [[Praetorian Guard|Elite Praetorian Guard]], causing their [[Force power|powers]] to become amplified as the bond between them grew stronger with every passing moment.

==Description==
{{Quote|On occasion, the Force allows us to connect with another living being and communicate with them across great distances, seeing what they see and feeling what they feel. Though this may seem like a harmless—and perhaps even valuable—ability, it is easily manipulated by those on the dark side. Some powerful Force users have been able to create secret bonds with others who are unaware of their connection. They then use those bonds to corrupt their target and steer their actions. Even if the unwanted bond is detected, it can still be extremely difficult to break.|Luke Skywalker|<ref name="Secrets of the Jedi">''[[Star Wars: The Secrets of the Jedi]]''</ref>}}
[[File:Luke_interrupting_Rey_and_Kylo_Ren.png|thumb|left|250px|A Force-bond connected two Force-sensitive individuals across space and time.]]
A Force-bond was a powerful [[Force power|connection]] between two [[Force-sensitive]] individuals that bridged their [[Brain|minds]],<ref name="Episode VIII" /> allowing them to communicate through the Force both visually and emotionally.<ref name="Secrets of the Jedi" /> The range of the bond was vast; it could span [[the galaxy]], keeping two beings united in a psychic link despite the physical distance between them.<ref name="TLJ:TVD">''[[Star Wars: The Last Jedi: The Visual Dictionary]]''</ref> As a result, both parties would [[Force vision|manifest]] in each other's location at the same [[time]],<ref name="Episode VIII" /> making it possible for them to see, hear and feel each other through their bond.<ref name="Secrets of the Jedi" /> In the rare occurrence of a [[Force dyad]], they were even capable of physical contact, therefore bridging the actual distance between them. The strength of the bond was determined by the level of their Force-sensitivity; the stronger they were in the Force, then the greater their bond became.<ref name="Rey Bio Gallery">{{Databank|url=rey-biography-gallery|title=Rey Biography Gallery}}</ref> In some cases, a bond or psychic link could cause at least one of the affected parties to suffer from aural and visual hallucinations.<ref name="Darth Maul">{{Databank|darth-maul|Darth Maul}}</ref>

==Application==
{{Quote|Why is the Force connecting us? You and I.|Kylo Ren, to Rey|<ref name="Episode VIII" />}}
[[File:Dark_side_ritual.png|thumb|right|250px|Darth Sidious and Darth Tyranus used the latter's bond with Grand Master Yoda to attack the will of the Jedi Order's leader.]]
Though the [[Jedi]] [[Luke Skywalker]] came to personally view them as an ability of the [[dark side of the Force]], Force-bonds were a neutral phenomenon that could exist between any kind of Force-user. Jedi, for example, often developed bonds between teacher and student.<ref name="Sacrifice" /> [[Avar Kriss]] of the [[High Republic Era]] was able to strengthen and draw on the power of Force-bonds to allow for feats impossible to be accomplished by a single Jedi.<ref name="Light of the Jedi" />

The creation of a [[lightsaber]], the most visible symbol of the [[Jedi Order]], required a [[kyber crystal]] that was bonded to a Force-sensitive individual. Kyber [[crystal]]s were rare gems that emitted a distinct presence in the Force; the Jedi sought out their own unique crystals and developed a Force connection with them in order to craft a lightsaber. The Force-bonded crystal's energy was unleashed by the lightsaber's energy cell.<ref name="Lightsaber Guide">''[[Star Wars Lightsabers: A Guide to Weapons of the Force]]''</ref>

Dark-side wielders could create secret bonds with others who were unaware of their connection, making it easier to corrupt the target and influence their judgment. Even if the unwanted bond was detected, it was extremely difficult to break.<ref name="Secrets of the Jedi" />

Though seemingly harmless and even advantageous,<ref name="Secrets of the Jedi" /> a bond could be used by a [[Sith Lord]] to manipulate the Force visions of a selected target through the use of a Force power known as a [[dark illusion]]. An ancient dark side ritual was required in order to harness the dark illusion. First, the practitioner had to draw [[DNA|blood]] from the person whom the target shared a bond with, compounded with an array of [[Force lightning]] in a sacrificial altar basin. At this stage of the ritual, the practitioner would chant an old [[Sith]] incantation in the [[Balc]] [[language]] while the bonded one concentrated on their bond with the intended victim. Once the ritual was complete, the Sith [[Lord]] would be able to peer into the mind of his target while controlling their visions to an extent.<ref name="Sacrifice" />

[[File:Snoke_chillin_like_a_villain.png|thumb|left|250px|Snoke manipulated the bond between Kylo Ren and Rey.]]
It was also possible for a Force-sensitive person to manipulate a bond<ref name="Episode VIII" /> that already existed<ref name="Kylo Ren Bio Gallery">{{Databank|kylo-ren-biography-gallery|Kylo Ren Biography Gallery}}</ref> between other Force-sensitives. A master of the Force could use their own power to bridge the minds of the bonded persons, allowing them to see, hear and touch each other regardless of the physical distance between them.<ref name="Episode VIII" /> However, they could also become connected by the Force itself, without the outside party's direct manipulation.<ref name="Kylo Ren Bio Gallery" />

Certain Force-based rituals could result in the creation of a psychic connection between two Force-sensitives,<ref name="Darth Maul" /> such as the [[Holocron fusion|fusion]] of two [[holocron]]s.<ref name="Visions and Voices">{{Rebels|Visions and Voices}}</ref> In order to combine a [[Jedi holocron]] with a [[Sith holocron]], the ritual required a light-side user and a wielder of the dark side to work together.<ref name="The Holocrons of Fate">{{Rebels|The Holocrons of Fate}}</ref> If the process was interrupted, the participants could become mentally linked with each other.<ref name="Visions and Voices" />

==Users==
===Avar Kriss===
A Jedi Master in the [[High Republic Era]], [[Avar Kriss]] held a rare ability amongst the Jedi Order by being able to strengthen the natural bonds between Force-sensitives. She could then form these bonds into a [[Song of the Force|Force network]]; though the Jedi could not speak to each other through this state, they could share emotional feelings, allowing them a form of communication. The Jedi in the network could "feel" each other in the back of their minds. Kriss used her ability to great effect during the [[Great Hyperspace Disaster]] and the [[Battle of Kur]].<ref name="Light of the Jedi">''[[The High Republic: Light of the Jedi]]''</ref>

===Dooku and Yoda===
{{Quote|Master Yoda has come to Moraband, although why, I do not know. We shall, however, use the strong bond that once existed between you and your master to allow us to see things that we otherwise could not.|Darth Sidious, to Darth Tyranus|<ref name="Sacrifice" />}}
[[File:YodaDookuDuelingDemo-AORDooku1.png|thumb|right|250px|A powerful bond once existed between Yoda and Dooku, long before the latter turned to the dark side of the Force.]]
Before turning to the dark side, [[Count of Serenno|Count]] [[Dooku]]—later known as [[Darth]] Tyranus during his [[Sith apprentice|apprenticeship]] to the [[Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth Sidious]]—was a Jedi Master who developed a strong Force-bond with [[Yoda]], the [[Grand Master]] who mentored him in the [[Jedi training|Jedi arts]]. During the [[Clone Wars]], Sidious detected Yoda's presence on the Sith [[homeworld]] of [[Moraband]]. Determined to learn the purpose of Yoda's visit, the Dark Lord also sensed an opportunity to strike a crippling blow to the Order by breaking the will of its oldest and wisest Jedi Master.<ref name="Sacrifice" /> At the [[time]], the [[Jedi High Council]] was uncertain if a Force user could be compromised by their bond with another being. [[Ki-Adi-Mundi]] suspected that it was possible, although his conjecture was doubted by [[Obi-Wan Kenobi]], who did not believe that a Jedi Master of Yoda's caliber could be affected by the dark side.<ref name="Voices">{{TCW|Voices}}</ref>

In order to affect Yoda's visions, Sidious required the assistance of someone who was once close to his intended target. To that end, he summoned Tyranus to [[Coruscant]] where they performed an old Sith ritual that enabled them to harness the power of dark illusion. By using Tyranus' Force-bond with Yoda, Sidious manufactured a Force vision designed to tempt Yoda with the dark side of the Force, resulting in a battle of wills between the Dark Lord and the Grand Master. Sidious' efforts to break Yoda's spirit ultimately failed, however. Yoda was strong, as noted by Sidious, and refused to be tempted by the dark side.<ref name="Sacrifice" />

===Ezra Bridger and Maul===
{{Quote|The dark-side wielder I told you about, the one who wanted to join the holocrons together… I've seen him here on Atollon.|Ezra Bridger, seeking answers from the Bendu in regard to his psychic link with Maul|<ref name="Visions and Voices" />}}
[[File:The_Holocrons_of_Fate_Episode_Guide.png|thumb|left|250px|Ezra Bridger and Maul combined their holocrons, forging a psychic link with each other in the process.]]
At the [[Imperial Era|height]] of the [[Galactic Empire]], [[Maul]]—a former Sith apprentice of Darth Sidious—sought the Jedi [[Ezra Bridger]]'s assistance in his quest to learn the fate of Obi-Wan Kenobi, the Jedi Master who [[Battle of Naboo|defeated]] Maul during the [[Invasion of Naboo|crisis]] on [[Naboo]]. Having [[Maul's capture of the Ghost crew|captured]] the young Padawan's [[Spectres|allies]], Maul was able to coerce Bridger's participation in a ritual involving the fusion of two holocrons, and thereby discovered that Kenobi had indeed survived the [[Great Jedi Purge]] of [[Order 66]]. However, a psychic link was also established between Maul and Bridger as a result of the ritual.<ref name="Darth Maul" />

Their newfound bond led Bridger to experience a series of aural hallucinations and Force visions relating to Maul;<ref name="Darth Maul" /> at one point on [[Atollon]], he nearly killed a [[Rebel trooper]] whom he mistook for the [[Dathomirian]]. After the incident, Bridger's Jedi Master, [[Kanan Jarrus]] brought him to the [[Bendu]], hoping to find a way to end the hallucinations that plagued his apprentice. By then, Maul had tracked their position and threatened to reveal the location of [[Chopper Base]] to the Empire unless Bridger agreed to finish the ritual on [[Dathomir]]. Bridger complied with the dark-side wielder's demands, both to ensure Maul's silence and to discover the key to destroying the Sith. Maul created a [[Merging potion|potion]] that merged their minds together, renewing their Force-bond. By [[Skirmish on Dathomir|finishing]] the ritual, Maul learned that Kenobi was on [[Tatooine]],<ref name="Visions and Voices" /> but was unable to find his exact location in the [[planet]]'s vast [[desert]]. He therefore used his connection with Bridger to [[Mission to Tatooine (Imperial Era)|lure]] the Padawan to Tatooine in order to draw his old nemesis out of hiding. Though he managed to find Kenobi with Bridger's unwitting aid, Kenobi ultimately defeated Maul in single combat to the death.<ref name="Twin Suns">{{Rebels|Twin Suns (episode)}}</ref>

===Kylo Ren and Rey===
{{Quote|Ben, when we touched hands I saw your future. Just the shape of it, but solid and clear.|Rey, referring to one of the effects of her bond with Kylo Ren|<ref name="Episode VIII" />}}
[[File:MindProbe-TFA.png|thumb|right|250px|A mysterious and unprecedented connection spanned across the galaxy to unite Kylo Ren and Rey.]]
A [[Doctrine of the Dyad|prophesied]] [[Force dyad|dyad in the Force]] was realized through the pairing of [[Ben Solo|Kylo Ren]] and [[Rey Skywalker|Rey]],<ref name="TROSVD">''[[Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary]]''</ref> [[Anakin Skywalker|Darth Vader's]] maternal [[Skywalker family|grandson]] and Darth Sidious' paternal [[Palpatine family|granddaughter]], respectively.<ref name="Episode IX">{{Film|IX}}</ref> Ren and Rey both possessed raw strength with the Force, and after the [[Battle of Starkiller Base|destruction]] of [[Starkiller Base]], they discovered<ref name="Episode VIII" /> a mysterious connection<ref name="Kylo Ren">{{Databank|kylo-ren|Supreme Leader Kylo Ren}}</ref> that intractably bridged their minds.<ref name="TROSVD" /> The bond allowed them to see and hear each other across millions of [[light-year]]s,<ref name="Rey Bio Gallery" /> with Ren located on the [[First Order Navy]] [[flagship]] ''[[Supremacy]]'' and the latter on the planet [[Ahch-To]].<ref name="Episode VIII" /> Their bond continued to grow stronger as Rey's powers increased,<ref name="Rey Bio Gallery" /> leading Ren to become mystified by the connection between them.<ref name="Kylo Ren" />

[[File:Rey_and_Ben_Solo.png|thumb|left|250px|Their bond was powerful, giving Rey insights into Ren that even Luke Skywalker could not see.]]
At first, Rey regarded their bond as an unwelcome development due to the series of Force visions that she shared with Ren, whom she begrudged for the [[Death|murder]] of [[Han Solo]], Ren's [[Parent|father]].<ref name="Episode VIII" /> However, she was surprised to see that Ren,<ref name="Rey Bio Gallery" /> whose emotional state was further affected by their bond,<ref name="Kylo Ren Bio Gallery" /> no longer tried to hide his hurt and pain from her.<ref name="Rey Bio Gallery" /> As time passed, Ren and Rey began to connect on a more intimate level, allowing her to sense his inner conflict<ref name="Episode VIII" /> and misery<ref name="Expanded Edition">''[[Star Wars: The Last Jedi: Expanded Edition]]''</ref> that caused him so much pain.<ref name="Kylo Ren" /> Their connection was unprecedented<ref name="TLJ:TVD" /> in its raw power,<ref name="Expanded Edition" /> and gave Rey insights into Ren that not even Luke Skywalker could see.<ref name="TLJ:TVD" /> As their connection grew stronger, Rey grew certain that their destinies were intertwined.<ref name="Rey">{{Databank|rey|Rey}}</ref>

Rey's inability to remember her parents led her to confide in Ren, who understood her feelings of isolation and abandonment. They assured each other that they were not alone, after which Rey reached to touch Ren's hand even though she expected that they would simply pass through each other while connected in the Force. Rey was surprised to discover that they could touch hands, during which they each experienced a Force vision. Rey's vision was of the future in which Ren turned back to the light side as Ben Solo, leading her to believe that his turn from the dark side could be the catalyst for shifting the tide of the conflict in the Resistance's favor. Ren's vision was of Rey's past in which he discovered the identities of her parents. As a result, Ren was convinced that Rey would willingly join him on the dark side after he revealed the truth about her past. Confident that her premonition would come to pass, Rey left Skywalker on Ahch-To to find Ren, believing that she could help him return to the light.<ref name="Expanded Edition" /> Although their bond provided Rey with insights into Ren that Skywalker did not perceive,<ref name="TLJ:TVD" /> Skywalker was unconvinced that his nephew could be saved. As such, Rey believed that the galaxy's last hope was Solo instead of Skywalker.<ref name="Episode VIII" />

[[File:TerryDodson-GreatestMomentsTextless.png|thumb|right|250px|Ren and Rey fought side by side, causing their Force-bond to grow stronger with every passing moment.]]
Rey [[Battle of Oetchi|confronted]] Ren on the ''Supremacy'' where she told him about the vision that she experienced during their last connection. Nevertheless, Ren brought her to the [[Snoke's throne room|throne room]] of his master, [[Supreme Leader]] [[Snoke]],<ref name="Episode VIII" /> who claimed<ref name="TROSVD" /> that he had "bridged their minds" in a plot to use Ren's emotional conflict to lure Rey into a trap.<ref name="Episode VIII" /> Snoke accomplished his plan by manipulating Ren's insecurity to his advantage, knowing he could not hide his conflict from Rey, who could not pass up the chance to redeem the former Ben Solo.<ref name="TLJA5">[[The Last Jedi Adaptation 5|''The Last Jedi Adaptation'' 5]]</ref> He encouraged Ren to complete his training in the dark side by executing Rey, only to be betrayed and killed by his own apprentice instead. Ren and Rey then stood with each other against Snoke's [[Praetorian Guard|Elite Praetorian Guards]],<ref name="Episode VIII" /> and as they fought together their bond grew stronger with their abilities mirroring and amplifying one other.<ref name="TROSVD" />

Their alliance was short-lived as Ren chose to take Snoke's place in spite of Rey's efforts to turn him to the light. However, he also intended to have Rey join him as rulers of a new galactic order. Therefore, Ren revealed the truth about Rey's lineage by telling her that her parents were mere junk traders who sold their daughter for money. He suspected that Rey suppressed the memory as a [[Youngling|child]], but had always known the truth on a subconscious level. Although Rey was tempted to accept Ren's offer,<ref name="Episode IX" /> she instead returned to the [[Resistance]]<ref name="Episode VIII" /> and continued her training as a Jedi,<ref name="Episode IX" /> leaving Ren to lead the First Order alone as the new Supreme Leader. In the aftermath of the [[Battle of Crait]], Ren and Rey briefly reconnected with each other through their Force-bond,<ref name="Episode VIII" /> although by then Ren sensed that Rey felt neither hatred nor compassion for him.<ref name="Expanded Edition" />

[[File:Force-bond_battle.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The bond was a result of Ren and Rey's shared nature as a dyad in the Force—two beings, who were one.]]
By the last [[Standard day|days]] of the [[First Order-Resistance War]], the bond between Ren and Rey became so strong that they were capable of transferring physical objects through the Force to each other's location.<ref name="Episode IX" /> On one occasion, Ren found his [[glove]] wet from [[Water|rain]] due to Rey's location on [[Ahch-To]].<ref name="Episode VIII" /> Ren used his connection with Rey to locate her on multiple occasions, first when he seized Rey's [[husk necklace]] that she [[Mission to Pasaana (Resistance)|acquired]] on [[Pasaana]], and later when their connection [[Mission to Kijimi|transported]] [[Darth Vader's armor|Darth Vader's mask]] from the [[Steadfast (Resurgent-class)|''Steadfast'']] to [[Kijimi]]. They were also capable of fighting each other through their bond despite being in separate locations.<ref name="Episode IX" /> Ren, who was unaware at first of the cause behind their bond,<ref name="TROSVD" /> discovered that they were a dyad in the Force, which he described as two beings who were one. Rey learned of their nature as dyad from Ren, who believed that it would cause her to join him, but Rey remained committed to the Resistance's cause.<ref name="Episode IX" />

[[File:BenandReyKiss-TROS.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Rey acknowledged her bond with Ben Solo through a kiss in the aftermath of their victory on Exegol.]]
Their [[Mission to Kef Bir|final duel]] ended with Ren rejecting the dark side and fighting alongside Rey as Ben Solo during the [[Battle of Exegol]].<ref name="Episode IX" /> Armed with only a [[blaster]], Solo was initially overpowered by his former followers, the [[Knights of Ren]], but after sensing his presence in the Force, Rey used their bond to transfer the [[Skywalker lightsaber]] to Solo.<ref name="TROS EE">''[[Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: Expanded Edition]]''</ref> Rey and Solo dispatched their remaining opponents before standing together<ref name="Episode IX" /> as Jedi<ref name="Skywalker: A Family at War">''[[Skywalker: A Family at War]]''</ref> against Darth Sidious, who, upon discovering their nature as a dyad, [[Force drain|drained]] the life force of their bond to heal the [[Cloning|cloned body]] that served as a vessel for his essence.<ref name="TROS EE" /> In spite of his increased power, Sidious was ultimately destroyed on [[Exegol]] but the battle also resulted in Rey's death. Solo therefore transferred his life force to Rey, sacrificing his life to revive her.<ref name="Episode IX" /> Before Solo's death, he and Rey shared a kiss in acknowledgment of the bond that they shared as a dyad.<ref name="TROS EE" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Force power known as "Force-bond" was first mentioned in "[[Voices]]," the eleventh episode of [[The Clone Wars: The Lost Missions|The Lost Missions]], which was released as the sixth season of the ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'' television series in [[March]] [[2014]].<ref name="Voices" /> Force-bond was first identified by name in the "[[Sacrifice (episode)|Sacrifice]]" episode gallery on [[StarWars.com]].<ref name="Episode Gallery">{{SW|url=series/clone-wars/sacrifice-episode-gallery|text="Sacrifice" Episode Gallery}}</ref>

It later appeared in the [[2015]] film ''[[Star Wars: Episode VII The Force Awakens]]'', the first installment of the Star Wars sequel trilogy.<ref name="Episode VII">{{Film|VII}}</ref><ref name="The Skywalker Legacy">{{HomeVideoCite|film=9|featurette=The Skywalker Legacy}}</ref> But it was first properly identified in the [[2017]] film [[Star Wars: Episode VIII The Last Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VIII ''The Last Jedi'']], the second film in the ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[sequel trilogy]], directed by [[Rian Johnson]], in which [[Ben Solo|Kylo Ren]] and [[Rey Skywalker|Rey]] experience a powerful connection with each other.<ref name="Episode VIII" />

According to Johnson, Ren and Rey's Force-bond in ''The Last Jedi'' was a storytelling device to allow them to speak to each other. If they met face to face, they were either going to fight or one of them would have to be tied up. Their Force sessions became a way to get Rey to go from hating Kylo Ren to actually engaging with him.<ref>{{WebCite|url=https://www.latimes.com/entertainment/movies/la-et-mn-star-wars-last-jedi-spoilers-20171218-htmlstory.html|text=Rian Johnson on the evolution of the Force in 'Star Wars: The Last Jedi' and more spoilers|date=2017-10-31|work=''[[Wikipedia:Los Angeles Times|Los Angeles Times]]''|archivedate=20191230222212}}</ref>

Rey and Kylo's Force-bond reappears in the [[2019]] film [[Star Wars: Episode IX The Rise of Skywalker]] and is revealed to be a [[Force dyad]]. [[Chris Terrio]], writer of ''The Rise of Skywalker'', states that Kylo Ren and Rey's unique Force-bond is [[the Force]] acting as fate to bring them together. Their bond is the equivalent as being soulmates in the Force, in which even if they are enemies on different sides of the war, they are able to understand each other because of their connection.<ref name="The Skywalker Legacy">{{HomeVideoCite|film=9|featurette=The Skywalker Legacy}}</ref>

In the novel ''[[The High Republic: Convergence]]'', Jedi Knight [[Gella Nattai]] is described as forming "a tether she had not yet understood was forming," but could not afford to yet sever, with [[Axel Greylark]]. Through their connection, Nattai heard the man call her name, but she was unsure if he consciously knew he was leading her to him. As she grew closer, she realized he was thinking her name so forcefully she could sense it. In truth, Greylark knew she would find him as part of his plot to destroy [[Eirami poison|Erami poison]].<ref name="Convergence">''[[The High Republic: Convergence]]''</ref>
